Details: Two full-scale studies measured natural organic matter removal by ozone as a pre-treatment prior to biological filtration. Removal of TOC and DOC by the ozonation process alone ranged from 2.6 to 31.2 percent and 2.1 to 30.8 percent, respectively [2442, 2452]. Ozonation actually increased BDOC and AOC, while removing the nonbiodegradable fraction of DOC, thus making the remaining fraction more amenable to removal by biological treatment [2442].
